# EXPORTERS

## Export Policy

There is one primary export target: an Omarchy Theme Directory.

Archive export and recipe export are secondary artifacts derived from the same validated Theme Model. They must not create parallel exporter architectures.

## Omarchy Theme Directory

Default local target:

`~/.config/omarchy/themes/<normalized-theme-name>`

The directory must be self-contained and usable as a theme repo root. Extracting an archive should produce a directory that can be used as `~/.config/omarchy/themes/<normalized-theme-name>` without restructuring.

## Generated Files

Required files:

- `colors.toml`;
- `backgrounds/<source-image>`;
- `preview.png`;
- `preview-unlock.png`;
- `unlock.png`;
- `neovim.lua`;
- `README.md`.

Conditional files:

- `icons.theme`: fixed/default value when emitted; may be omitted to allow Omarchy fallback;
- `light.mode`: only for explicit light-theme exports;
- recipe file: only when explicitly requested;
- any future file only when Omarchy expects it and does not template it.

Do not directly generate files that installed Omarchy templates can generate from `colors.toml`.

## Installed Omarchy Templates

When Omarchy is installed, installed templates and local script behavior are the source of truth.

Export writes the source facts Omarchy expects:

- `colors.toml`;
- background assets;
- preview/unlock assets;
- direct files Omarchy expects but does not template.

When Omarchy is not installed, export may still generate from the documented contract, but validation confidence is reduced and apply is unavailable.

## Archive Export

Archive export is explicit.

Modes:

- finished-theme archive: exported theme directory content only;
- reproducible archive: theme directory, recipe, and source image bytes after confirmation and privacy warning.

Archive rules:

- preserve normalized theme directory name;
- make archive root the theme directory;
- do not write to `~/.config/omarchy/themes` unless local export is also requested;
- do not add license, `.gitignore`, git metadata, or publishing metadata.

## Recipe Export

Recipe export is explicit and never automatic.

Recipe must include:

- generator version;
- source image fingerprint;
- generation seed/style option;
- selection mode;
- selected directions/groups/surfaces;
- enough data for non-interactive CLI reproduction.

Recipe may bundle source image bytes only after confirmation and privacy warning.

Recipes are not history. The product must not maintain a session database.

## README

Every exported theme includes `README.md`.

README must:

- state the theme was generated by `omarchy-themegen`;
- explain installation of `omarchy-themegen`, including `go install`;
- show basic CLI usage;
- explain how to install and apply the exported Omarchy theme;
- avoid claiming Omarchy requires the README.

README may include generation details only when the user opts in.

## Validation

Before writing:

- source image validation passed;
- user explicitly provided a theme name;
- normalized theme name is non-empty;
- exactly one Theme Model exists;
- component-mix composition has been revalidated;
- recipe input, when used, has been parsed and validated;
- all required color keys exist;
- all colors are valid `#RRGGBB`;
- required assets can be generated;
- `magick` is available for operations that require it.

After writing:

- target directory exists;
- required files exist;
- `colors.toml` contains all required keys;
- optional `omarchy theme list` check can see the theme when Omarchy is installed.

Validation is structural and non-mutating. It must not run `omarchy theme set`.

## Overwrite And Rollback

Default: refuse overwrite.

Confirmed replacement:

- shows existing path;
- creates a timestamped backup;
- writes replacement only after backup succeeds;
- leaves backup in place unless the user explicitly cleans it up.

`--yes` may confirm overwrite only when backup creation is included. It must not imply apply.

## Apply

Apply is separate from export.

Running `omarchy theme set <name>` requires explicit confirmation or an explicit CLI apply option. Generic `--yes` must not trigger apply.

Apply delegates to Omarchy. The product must not recreate Omarchy's symlink, restart, hook, or template behavior.

## Publishing

The product stops at a theme-repo-compatible folder/archive.

It must not:

- create remote repositories;
- push to GitHub;
- authenticate with hosting providers;
- choose a license;
- generate publishing metadata.
